返回本批次

今晚整个文明可能会灭亡

A whole civilization might die tonight

www.nbcnews.com 作者 hedayet 分数 136 评论 92 2026/04/08 01:31
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 周二晚上，唐纳德·特朗普总统宣布与伊朗停火两周，就在他威胁“整个文明今晚将灭亡”的几个小时后，距离他为德黑兰设定的与美国达成协议的最后期限大约 90 分钟。他在《Truth Social》上写道：“立即安全地开放霍尔木兹海峡，我同意暂停对伊朗的轰炸和袭击两周。”当天早些时候，特朗普在社交媒体上警告称，如果两个交战国家之间没有达成协议，“整个文明今晚将消亡”。这一威胁是特朗普迄今为止在冲突中最极端的公开言论，其中包括发誓要因伊朗持续中断航运而对伊朗基础设施发动攻击在多次延长伊朗重新开放这条重要航线的最后期限后，特朗普于晚上 8 点宣布，这条航线通常占世界石油和天然气产量的五分之一。美国东部时间周二是伊朗达成协议的最后期限。周日，特朗普在一篇帖子中威胁伊朗，如果伊朗在周二最后期限前不重新开放海峡，他会使用脏话并援引伊斯兰教。这是特朗普对德黑兰一系列不断升级的威胁中的最新一次，包括威胁要轰炸该国进入“石器时代”，称伊朗政府为“疯狂的混蛋”，同时要求其开放关键的航运特朗普周二上午的威胁立即引发了民主党人的强烈谴责，他们表示，这种对基础设施的袭击将构成战争罪。“唐纳德·特朗普完全精神错乱了。他威胁要消灭整个文明的言论震惊了良心，需要国会做出果断的回应，”众议院少数党领袖、纽约州民主党人哈基姆·杰弗里斯在与其领导团队的联合声明中表示。 “在唐纳德·特朗普将我们的国家拖入第三次世界大战之前，众议院必须立即重新开会，投票结束这场在中东的鲁莽选择战争。”参议院少数党领袖、纽约州民主党人查克·舒默和参议院主要委员会的一群民主党高级成员发表了一份罕见的联合声明，警告特朗普“绝不能兑现这一威胁。”“我们今天以一个声音和一个目的发言：谴责特朗普总统消灭整个文明的威胁，”参议员们说。 “故意破坏数千万平民赖以生存的电力、水源或基本基础设施来惩罚那些在伊朗政权手中受苦受难的平民，将构成战争罪、对这个国家赖以建立的价值观的背叛和道德上的失败。”前中央情报局分析员、密歇根州民主党人艾丽莎·斯洛特金曾警告说，大规模针对平民“将明显违反《日内瓦公约》以及五角大楼《战争法手册》中规定的武装冲突法”，她再次呼吁军人拒绝非法命令。“我希望并相信，如果我们的部队——尤其是指挥人员——在接到明显非法的命令时，将有道德上的清醒来反击，”斯洛特金在一份声明中说。与此同时，在特朗普周二上午发表声明后，国会中的大多数共和党领导人几乎没有说什么。众议院议长迈克·约翰逊 (R-La.) 和参议院多数党领袖约翰·图恩 (R-S.D.) 的办公室没有回复寻求置评的消息。参议院共和党 X 官方账户发表了一篇未署名的帖子，称：“伊朗明智的做法是相信特朗普总统的话。他们可以选择简单的方法，也可以选择困难的方法。” （该账号由阿肯色州共和党参议员汤姆·科顿（Tom Cotton）的工作人员负责。）尽管如此，一些“MAGA”影响者和特朗普盟友批评了特朗普周二早些时候在“真相社会”上发表的帖子，并在最近几天越来越多地反对他的伊朗政策。威斯康星州共和党人罗恩·约翰逊周一表示，他不希望看到美国袭击伊朗的民用基础设施，这标志着与特朗普的罕见决裂。“我希望并祈祷特朗普总统，这确实是虚张声势。我不希望看到我们开始炸毁民用基础设施，”约翰逊在“约翰·所罗门报告”播客的一集中说道。“我不想看到这种情况。我们没有与伊朗人民交战。我们正在努力解放伊朗人民。”他补充道。前佐治亚州众议员马乔里·泰勒·格林（Marjorie Taylor Greene）是特朗普的盟友，后来成为批评者，她呼吁通过第 25 条修正案将特朗普赶下台。“第 25 条修正案！！！”她在停火宣布之前在 X 上发帖。 “没有一颗炸弹落在美国。我们无法杀死整个文明。这是邪恶和疯狂的。”第 25 条修正案规定了总统继任计划，以及副总统和成员的程序。如果总统“无法履行其办公室的权力和职责”，内阁可能会在国会的参与下罢免总统。特朗普周二上午通过电话与福克斯新闻的布雷特·拜尔进行了交谈。 “他说是晚上 8 点，”拜尔在广播中转述道。 “这就是他所说的。他说，如果我们到了那个地步，就会发生他们从未见过的袭击。……现在，他说，如果今天谈判取得进展，并且有一些具体的事情，那可能会改变，但在这个时候，他不想对此提出质疑，但他说，我们正在推进我们现有的计划。” 特朗普在周一的新闻发布会上反复威胁该国的基础设施，他告诉记者，美国有一项计划，“每座桥梁都将被拆除”。到明天晚上 12 点，伊朗的所有发电厂都将被摧毁，伊朗的所有发电厂都将停业——燃烧、爆炸，再也无法使用。“周一德黑兰袭击后，烟雾升起。阿塔·凯纳雷/法新社 - 盖蒂图片社人权观察组织前执行主任肯尼思·罗斯 (Kenneth Roth) 表示，他指出，在武装冲突期间对平民进行集体惩罚违反了《日内瓦第四公约》。“以恐吓平民为目的而发出威胁也是一种战争罪。”他解释说，根据国际人道主义法，威胁实施战争罪本身就可能构成战争罪。一些普通民主党参议员批评特朗普周二上午的帖子“精神错乱”。其他人，包括马萨诸塞州参议员埃德·马基和密歇根州众议员拉希达·特莱布，呼吁弹劾特朗普或将其免职。即使在总统周二晚上宣布为期两周的停火协议后，马基仍重申他推动特朗普被免职，其他几位国会民主党人也呼吁采取类似行动。纽约州的亚历山大·奥卡西奥-科尔特斯、新墨西哥州的梅兰妮·斯坦斯伯里和马萨诸塞州的塞斯·莫尔顿在自己的社交媒体帖子中表示，无论停火如何，特朗普的言论都值得被免职。“无论是否暂时停火，特朗普都已经犯下了可弹劾的罪行，”莫尔顿在 X 上写道。“国会需要重新开始工作，在他对我们的国家和世界造成更多损害之前将他免职。”舒默做到了周二晚上，他没有呼吁特朗普下台，而是在一篇帖子中表示，“我很高兴特朗普退缩了，并且正在拼命寻找摆脱他荒谬的咆哮的任何出口坡道。”

Original Summary

  1. President Donald Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran on Tuesday night — hours after he'd threatened "a whole civilization will die tonight" and about 90 minutes before a deadline he set for Tehran to reach an agreement with the U.S.“Based on conversations with Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Field Marshal Asim Munir, of Pakistan, and wherein they requested that I hold off the destructive force being sent tonight to Iran, and subject to the Islamic Republic of Iran agreeing to the COMPLETE, IMMEDIATE, and SAFE OPENING of the Strait of Hormuz, I agree to suspend the bombing and attack of Iran for a period of two weeks,” he wrote on Truth Social.Earlier in the day, Trump had warned on social media that "a whole civilization will die tonight" if there was no deal between the two warring countries.That threat, Trump's most extreme public rhetoric in the conflict to date, had included a vow to launch attacks on Iran's infrastructure over its continued disruption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.After he extended the deadline multiple times for Iran to reopen the vital shipping route, which about a fifth of the world's oil and natural gas usually passes through, Trump announced 8 p.m. ET Tuesday as the final deadline for Iran to come to a deal.In a post Sunday, Trump threatened Iran's power plants and bridges if it did not reopen the strait by the Tuesday deadline, using expletives and invoking Islam.It was the latest in a series of escalating threats by Trump against Tehran, including threatening to bomb the country into “the Stone Ages” and calling the Iranian government “crazy bastards” while demanding that it open up the key shipping route.Trump’s threat Tuesday morning sparked a wave of fierce and immediate condemnations from Democrats, who said such strikes on infrastructure would constitute war crimes.“Donald Trump is completely unhinged. His statement threatening to eradicate an entire civilization shocks the conscience and requires a decisive congressional response,” House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, D-N.Y., said in a joint statement with his leadership team. “The House must come back into session immediately and vote to end this reckless war of choice in the Middle East before Donald Trump plunges our country into World War III.”Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, D-N.Y., and a group of Democratic ranking members of key Senate committees issued a rare joint statement warning Trump that he “must not follow through on this threat.”“We speak today with one voice and one purpose: to condemn President Trump’s threat to extinguish an entire civilization,” the senators said. “Intentionally destroying the power, water, or basic infrastructure upon which tens of millions of civilians depend to punish the very civilians who suffer at the hands of the Iranian regime would constitute a war crime, a betrayal of the values this nation was founded on, and a moral failure.”Sen. Elissa Slotkin, D-Mich., a former CIA analyst, had warned that targeting civilians on a mass scale “would be a clear violation of the law of armed conflict as laid out in the Geneva Conventions, as well as the Pentagon’s Law of War Manual,” and she revived her calls for service members to refuse illegal orders.“I hope and believe our troops — especially those in command — will have the moral clarity to push back if they are given clearly illegal orders,” Slotkin said in a statement.Meanwhile, most GOP leaders in Congress had little to say following Trump’s statement Tuesday morning. The offices of House Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., and Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D., didn’t reply to messages seeking comment.The official Senate Republican X account published an unsigned post saying: “Iran would be wise to take President Trump at his word. They can choose the easy way or the hard way.” (The handle is run by the staff of Sen. Tom Cotton, R-Ark.)Still, a number of MAGA influencers and Trump allies criticized Trump’s post earlier Tuesday on Truth Social and have increasingly pushed back against his Iran policy in recent days.Sen. Ron Johnson, R-Wis., said Monday that he didn't want to see the U.S. attack Iran’s civilian infrastructure, marking a rare break with Trump.“I am hoping and praying that President Trump, that this really is bluster. I do not want to see us start blowing up civilian infrastructure,” Johnson said on an episode of the “John Solomon Reports” podcast.“I do not want to see that. We are not at war with the Iranian people. We are trying to liberate them,” he added.Former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene, of Georgia, a Trump ally-turned-critic, called for Trump to be removed through the 25th Amendment.“25TH AMENDMENT!!!” she posted on X before the ceasefire announcement. “Not a single bomb has dropped on America. We cannot kill an entire civilization. This is evil and madness.”The 25th Amendment lays out the presidential succession plan, as well as a process for the vice president and members of the Cabinet to remove the president from office, potentially with the involvement of Congress, if the president “is unable to discharge the powers and duties of his office.”Trump spoke to Fox News' Bret Baier by phone Tuesday morning. "He said 8 p.m. is happening," Baier relayed on the air. "That’s what he said. He said it is — if we get to that point, there is going to be an attack like they have not seen. ... Now, he said if negotiations move forward today and there is something concrete, that could change, but at this hour — he didn’t want to put odds on it — but he said it is moving forward with the plans that we have."Trump repeated threats against the country's infrastructure at a news conference Monday, telling reporters that the U.S. has a plan "where every bridge in Iran will be decimated by 12 o’clock tomorrow night, where every power plant in Iran will be out of business — burning, exploding and never to be used again."Smoke rises following strikes Monday in Tehran.Atta Kenare / AFP - Getty ImagesInternational law experts concurred with Democrats that Trump's threats against things like power stations would be potential war crimes if they were carried out.“Trump is openly threatening collective punishment, targeting not the Iranian military but the Iranian people,” said Kenneth Roth, the former executive director of Human Rights Watch, noting that collective punishment of civilians during armed conflict is a violation of the Fourth Geneva Convention.“Attacking civilians is a war crime. So is making threats with the aim of terrorizing the civilian population,” he said, explaining that threatening to carry out a war crime is potentially a war crime itself under international humanitarian law.Some rank-and-file Democratic senators criticized Trump’s post Tuesday morning as “unhinged.” Others, including Sen. Ed Markey, of Massachusetts, and Rep. Rashida Tlaib, of Michigan, called for Trump’s impeachment or removal from office.Markey reiterated his push for Trump's removal even after the president announced the two-week ceasefire deal Tuesday evening, with several other congressional Democrats calling for similar action.Reps. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, of New York, Melanie Stansbury, of New Mexico, and Seth Moulton, of Massachusetts, said in their own social media posts that regardless of a ceasefire, Trump's remarks warranted removal from office."Temporary ceasefire or not, Trump already committed an impeachable offense," Moulton wrote on X. "Congress needs to get back to work and remove him from office before he does more damage to our country and the world."Schumer did not call for Trump's removal on Tuesday evening but instead said in a post, "I’m glad Trump backed off and is desperately searching for any sort of exit ramp from his ridiculous bluster."

评论（默认中文）

jghn 2026/04/08 01:43
在很多方面，我感觉到他的言论意味着核攻击埋葬了莱德。这不太可能发生。

更有可能发生的情况也同样糟糕。破坏性的民用基础设施将使数十万人面临生命危险。

即使人们从以美国为中心的短视视角来看待这一问题，有没有人考虑过，我们被告知需要解放的这些人中有多少人现在变得激进起来反对我们？

In a lot of ways I feel the discourse that he means a nuclear attack buries the lede. This is not likely to happen.

What is more likely to happen is just as bad. Devastating civil infrastructure would put 10s of millions of lives at risk.

And even if one were to look at this from a myopic USA centric lens, has anyone considered how many of these people we were told needed liberation are now radicalized against us?

在 HN 中查看
CommanderData 2026/04/08 02:00
用辐射污染该地区，包括以色列的供应链和出口，这并不是我的宾果卡上的事情。

我猜以色列只会从美国进口所有东西，并期望 AIPAC 在其自己的农业部门遭受重创后履行其增加资金的职责。

Contaminating the region with radiation including Israels supply chain and exports wasn't something on my bingo cards.

I guess Israel would just import everything from the US, and expect AIPAC to do it's job at increasing funding once it's own farming sector has been decimated.

在 HN 中查看
Animats 2026/04/08 02:29
伊朗的回应：“伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队（IRGC）表示，如果美国跨越“红线”并袭击民用设施，它将在该地区之外做出反应，并剥夺美国及其盟友“多年”的石油和天然气供应。”[1]

据国营《德黑兰时报》报道，“伊朗已关闭与美国的所有外交和间接沟通渠道……”美国媒体似乎没有注意到这一点，但印度和中国的媒体却注意到了。 [2]但共同的来源似乎是《德黑兰时报》，目前还不清楚是谁在运营该报，也不清楚他们从哪里获得信息。 《纽约时报》、美联社和半岛电视台并没有这么说。新华社有一篇没有来源的文字说明。美国白宫称万斯正在与某人交谈。 Politico 称万斯处于“待命状态”。[3]

现在看来不太可能通过谈判达成停火。

[1] https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/4/7/iran-war-li...

[2] https://www.the-independent.com/bulletin/news/iran-mediators...

[3] https://www.politico.com/news/2026/04/06/vance-is-on-standby...

Response from Iran: "Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) says it will respond outside the region and deprive the United States and its allies of oil and gas “for many years” if the US crosses “red lines” and attacks civilian facilities."[1]

"Iran has closed all diplomatic and indirect channels of communication with the United States, the state-run Tehran Times reported ..." US media does not seem to have picked up on this, but media in India and China have.[2] But the common source seems to be "Tehran Times", and it's unclear who runs that or where they get their info. New York Times, AP, and AlJazeera are not saying that. Xinhua has a one-line note with no source. The US White House says Vance is talking to somebody. Politico says Vance is on "standby".[3]

A negotiated cease-fire seems unlikely now.

[1] https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/4/7/iran-war-li...

[2] https://www.the-independent.com/bulletin/news/iran-mediators...

[3] https://www.politico.com/news/2026/04/06/vance-is-on-standby...

nailer 2026/04/08 05:10
> 伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队（IRGC）表示将在该地区之外做出回应

如何？ IGRC 在伊朗境内艰难生存。

> Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) says it will respond outside the region

How? The IGRC is struggling to survive inside Iran.

comrade1234 2026/04/08 03:31
市场下跌。他和他的亲信正在买入股票。明天他会说伊朗正在谈判并提供一些令人惊奇的东西。股票会上涨，他们会卖出。伊朗会说他们不知道他在说什么。清洗并重复。
The markets dropped. He and his cronies are buying up stocks. Tomorrow he'll say Iran is negotiating and offering something amazing. The stocks will go up and they'll sell. Iran will say they have no idea what he's talking about. Wash and repeat.

pan69 2026/04/08 04:13
尽管听起来令人沮丧，但这似乎概括了第二任特朗普政府的目标。
As depressing as it sounds, this seems to pretty much sum up the objective of the 2nd Trump admin.

hedayet 2026/04/08 02:28
正如预期的那样 - 这篇文章被标记了。

如此严重的生存危机不能在 HN 上讨论吗？

As anticipated - this post got flagged.

An existential crisis as big as this can't be discussed on HN?

在 HN 中查看
srean 2026/04/08 03:24
如果很多人“担保”它，它就会被取消标记。
If many people 'vouch' for it, it will get unflagged.
在 HN 中查看
sph 2026/04/08 03:36
如果您“担保”或“标记”模组不批准的内容，它就会在您的帐户中被禁用。

在与 dang [1] 的私人谈话中证实，在我承诺表现良好后，他恢复了它，但现在它又停止工作了，因为我没有正确遵守规则。

把权力交给用户，并默认你与审核团队有相同的观点和政治倾向，这是相当虚伪的。

---

1：引用：“恐怕我们从您的帐户中剥夺了担保特权，因为您担保了太多不具实质性和/或煽动性和/或以其他方式违反网站准则的评论”

If you 'vouch' or 'flag' things the mods don't approve of, it gets disabled on your account.

Confirmed in private conversation with dang [1], he restored it after I promised to behave, and now it stopped working again as I didn't properly toe the line.

Quite hypocritical to give power to the user with the tacit expectation that you share the same opinions and political bent as the moderation team.

---

1: quote: "I'm afraid we took vouching privileges away from your account because you vouched for too many comments that were unsubstantive and/or flamebait and/or otherwise broke the site guidelines"

在 HN 中查看
Henchman21 2026/04/08 09:27
这个网站就是个笑话。它不会促进讨论，​​而是促进“允许的”讨论。那些让 Y-combinator 成为人们的东西 更多的钱 因为这就是他们所看重的。技术只是达到目的的手段。而这些“网站指南”的概念似乎根本不适用于埃隆的人！

所以，你知道它就像任何右翼拥有的新闻网站一样。

This site is a fucking joke. It doesn't foster discussion, rather it fosters "allowed" discussion. Things that will make the Y-combinator folks more money because that is all they value. Tech is just a means to an end. And these notions of "site guidelines" seem to not apply at all to Elon's folks!

So, you know it pretty much like any right-wing-owned news site.

在 HN 中查看
nkurz 2026/04/08 03:30
不幸的是，这不是它的工作方式。 如果它由于用户标记而被[标记][死亡]，一些用户可以保证它能够恢复。

但如果它只是被[标记]（相关处罚使其远离头版），则无法保证消除处罚。

该项目当前已被[标记]，无法提供担保选项。

Unfortunately that's not the way it works. If it's [flagged] [dead] because of user flagging, some users can vouch for it to revive it.

But if it's merely [flagged] (with associated penalties keeping it off the front pages) there is no option for vouching to remove the penalty.

This one is currently [flagged] with no option for vouching.

nailer 2026/04/08 05:28

It just draws foolish people that support the IGRC (because the IGRC hates Jews and so do they) and want to use HN for left wing advocacy.
在 HN 中查看

在 HN 中查看
twen_ty 2026/04/08 02:05
当没有有效的反对时就会发生这种情况。民主党在哪里？媒体在哪里？
This is what happens when there's no effective opposition. Where are the dems? Where's the press?
在 HN 中查看
hedayet 2026/04/08 02:39
我还想问我们普通的乔和简可以做什么来避免这场灾难？

除了走出去，举着标语牌站在街上之外，我想不出什么别的办法。我们还能做些什么比这更好的事情吗？

I'd also ask what can we regular Joes and Janes do to avoid this disaster?

I can't think of anything other than going out and standing on a street with a placard. can we do anything better than that?

在 HN 中查看
nathanaldensr 2026/04/08 02:52
问题不在于必须做什么；而在于。从美国建国之初就可以找到关于这个话题的智慧。唯一的问题是，人们有集体勇气去做吗？
The question isn't what must be done; look to the very founding of the United States for wisdom on that topic. The only question is, do people have the collective courage to do it?
在 HN 中查看
jghn 2026/04/08 02:11
他们到底应该在这里做什么？
What are they supposed to do here, exactly?
在 HN 中查看
jacquesm 2026/04/08 02:11
全国范围的罢工将是一个良好的开始。
Country wide strike would be a nice start.
在 HN 中查看
sheikhnbake 2026/04/08 02:14
这需要几十年来一直被暴力镇压的阶级意识。尽管也许有一些希望基于不断打破国王人数的记录
That would require a level of class consciousness that has been violently suppressed for decades. Although maybe there's some hope based on continual record breaking no kings numbers
在 HN 中查看
TimorousBestie 2026/04/08 02:17

Managed opposition requires qualitatively different resources than a general strike.

在 HN 中查看
srean 2026/04/08 02:14
这是以色列的战争，美国民主党传统上一直非常支持。最后一个地方期待任何像你所要求的事情。
This is Israel's war and the American democrats have traditionally been very supportive. Last place to expect anything like what you are calling for.
在 HN 中查看
elzbardico 2026/04/08 06:43
对于以色列可以走多远，民主党有明确的红线。巴勒斯坦？当然，你他妈想做什么就做什么。

伊朗战争会导致全球经济危机吗？天哪，不！比比闭嘴！

从本质上讲，这就是民主党和如今自称为共和党的实体之间的区别。

The dems had clear red lines in how far Israel could go. Palestine? sure, do whatever the fuck you want.

Iran war that would lead to a fucking global economic crisis? Hell no! shut up Bibi!

That's in a gist the difference between the Democrats and the entity who call themselves the Republican party nowadays.

在 HN 中查看
jacquesm 2026/04/08 02:20
不，现在这是美国的战争。以色列参与其中，但美国是目前的主要交战方，如果特朗普兑现他的威胁，他几乎肯定会导致美国垮台。这已经走得太远了，由于美国的内部制衡似乎不起作用，因此将由世界其他国家划定界限。我们不能让这场战争演变成另一场世界大战，尤其是涉及多个核国家的情况下。

可以这样想：伊朗在世界舞台上被孤立。但如果它成为一个足够重要的受害者，那么战争的双方都会有两个主要宗教。这是一个可能让二战看起来“温和”的秘诀。我知道有人确实想要这个，但至少让我们清楚地看到：没有人，我的意思是绝对没有人会从中受益。即使是那些认为自己会受益的人。希望支持这一点的民主党人是少数，以便理智能够占上风。

想想雪崩：你可以引发雪崩，但你无法阻止雪崩。雪崩会自行停止，但只有当所有潜在能量都耗尽时。

No, this is now America's war. Israel has a part in it but the US is the main belligerent at this point and if Trump follows through on his threats he all but guarantees the downfall of the United States. This has gone way too far already and since the internal checks and balances of the United States appear not to be working it will be up to the rest of the world to draw a line. We can not afford to let this spiral into another world war, especially not with multiple nuclear powers involved.

Think of it this way: Iran is isolated on the world stage. But turn it into a significant enough victim and there will be two major religions on either side of a war. That's a recipe for something that could potentially make WWII look 'mild'. I know there are people that actually want this, but let's at least see clearly here: nobody, and I mean absolutely nobody will benefit from that. Even the ones who think they will benefit. Democrats that are supportive of this are hopefully a small enough minority that sanity will prevail.

Think avalanche: you can start one, but you can't stop one. Avalanches stop themselves, but only when all potential energy is expended.

在 HN 中查看
srean 2026/04/08 02:59
我同意。

我所说的以色列战争的意思是，动机是以色列的，他们让美国人成为他们的傀儡。

我认为除非欧洲表现出更多的强硬态度，否则这种局势不会很快缓和。

那里几乎是噩梦般的燃料。

I agree.

What I mean by Israel's war is that the motivation was Israel's, they got Americans to be their grunts.

I don't see this de-escalating soon unless Europe shows more teeth.

It's pretty much nightmare fuel out there.

在 HN 中查看
jacquesm 2026/04/08 03:01
> 我认为除非欧洲表现出更多的强硬态度，否则这种局势不会很快缓和。

你能描述一下你的想法吗？

> I don't see this de-escalating soon unless Europe shows more teeth.

Can you describe what you have in mind?

在 HN 中查看
srean 2026/04/08 03:09
除非特朗普察觉到新的意外威胁，否则我认为他不会退缩。俄罗斯和中国的反对已得到考虑。

与毫无说服力的外交演讲相比，欧洲更加好战的反对派可能会给他们一个暂停的理由。比如说，取消主要受益者是美国的主要合同。

不知道是否会发生，但很高兴看到一些欧洲国家表明立场，并用更多的言语来支持他们的立场。

Unless Trump perceives a new unexpected threat I don't see him backing down. Russian and Chinese opposition has been accounted for.

Europe's more belligerent opposition as opposed toothless diplomatic speeches may give them a reason to pause. Say, cancellations of major contracts where the primary beneficiary would have been the US.

Don't know whether it will happen, but am happy to see some European countries to take a stand and back their stand with more than words.

在 HN 中查看
jacquesm 2026/04/08 03:13
欧洲有一种可以使用的“武器”，但只能使用一次：抛售债券。问题在于，你需要一个足够大的理由，以至于当你要这么做的时候，可能已经太晚了，无法发挥作用，而且作为一种惩罚措施，它没有什么意义，离宣战只有一步之遥。如果特朗普继续这种疯狂行为，那么无论如何，明天早上世界经济都将陷入混乱。请注意，我们来到这里表面上是为了“解放伊朗人民”，显然他们需要被谋杀才能获得自由。太奇怪了。
Europe has one 'weapon' they can use but they can use it just once: dump the bonds. The problem with that is that you need a reason big enough that by the time you're going to do it it will likely be too late to be effective and as a punitive measure it makes little sense, it's just one step short of a declaration of war. If Trump goes ahead with this madness then tomorrow morning the world economy will be in shambles, no matter what. Note that we got here ostensibly to 'free the Iranian people', apparently they need to be murdered to make them free. It's grotesque.
在 HN 中查看
srean 2026/04/08 03:17
这是为了你和我。并不是每个人都这么看。

当有一天尘埃落定时，希望是良性的，我希望能够吸取一些关于选举什么样的领导人的教训。

这让我非常不安，以至于几周来我几乎无法完成很多工作。我喜欢做的工作。然后我想到伊朗公民，并为抱怨而感到内疚。

It is to you and me. Not everyone sees it that way.

When one day the dust settles, hopefully of the benign kind, I wish some lessons to be learned on what kind of leaders to elect.

This has had me so disturbed, that I have barely been able to get much work done for weeks. Work that I enjoy doing. Then I think of Iranian citizens and feel guilty about complaining.

在 HN 中查看
jacquesm 2026/04/08 03:21
至少我们对第二次世界大战如何爆发的问题有了答案。所需要的只是几个白痴，他们认为自己都很强大，舞台已经搭建好了。
At least we have answers to the question of how World War II started. All it takes is a couple of idiots that think they are all powerful and the stage is set.
在 HN 中查看
elzbardico 2026/04/08 06:46
特朗普是婴儿潮一代，成长于美国实力无可争议的时代。其次，他是一个自恋者，在他让别人称他为失败者之前，他会让罗马被烧毁。

第三。即使我们的老人有更清醒的头脑，我怀疑他是否能够控制以色列。让他们单独与伊朗作战，他们的导弹防御系统已经耗尽，让伊朗的导弹如雨点般落在各处，比比肯定会用核武器攻击德黑兰和也门，所以我什至不认为有什么出路。

Trump is a boomer who grow in a age where American power was incontested. Second, he is a narcisist and he will let Rome burn down before he let someone call him a loser.

Third. Even if our gerontocrat had a saner mind, I doubt he would be able to control Israel. Let them alone fighting Iran, with their missile defense depleted leting Iranian missiles rain down everywhere, Bibi would surely nuke Tehran and the Yemen, so I don't even think there's a way out of this.

在 HN 中查看
srean 2026/04/08 10:41
如果比比想要在没有美国支持的情况下使用核武器，那就让他尝试一下吧。他会因为结束以色列或乌克兰或两者而被铭记。
If Bibi wants to nuke without US's blessings just let him try. He would remembered for ending Israel or Ukraine or both.
在 HN 中查看
pipo234 2026/04/08 02:31
技术术语似乎是： 诱捕。战争本来可以在最初的几天“成功”之后结束。

但这种情况每天都在继续，想要退出就变得有点困难。就像越南、伊拉克或阿富汗一样，这场战争不可能获胜。

The technical term seems to be: entrapment. The war could have ended after the first few "successful" days.

And still every day it continues, it becomes a little bit harder to back out. Like Vietnam, Iraq or Afghanistan this war cannot be won.

dylan604 2026/04/08 02:39

dylan604 2026/04/08 05:01
这需要 15 名内阁成员援引第 25 条，随后国会两院 2/3 的议员同意。所以，是的，在这种情况下，没有足够的脊柱参与到 25 日
That requires that 15 cabinet members invoke the 25th followed by 2/3 of both house of Congress agree. So, yeah, not enough spine involved to count on the 25th in this situation

msabalau 2026/04/08 02:30
在哪里 你？

除了发布假装国会两院少数党实际上可以阻止总统的内容之外，你到底在做什么，除非占多数的政客选择加入他们。

尽管媒体的报道平淡无奇，但事实是调查显示美国人普遍不赞成这场战争。 大概是因为他们从媒体和反对派那里了解到了这一点。

负责的人是当权者及其剩余的支持者。可以说，这是受到那些拥护愤世嫉俗、自我安慰的抱怨的人的帮助，这些抱怨忽视了大多数人在中学学到的公民知识。

Where are you?

What precisely are you doing other than posting stuff that pretends that a party in minority in both houses of Congress could actually stop a president, unless some politicians in the majority chose to join them.

And, as underwhelming as the press are, the facts are that surveys show that American's broadly disapprove of the war. Presumably because of what they have learned about it from the press and the opposition.

The people who are responsible are the people in power and their remaining supporters. Aided, arguably, by people who espouse cynical, self-soothing complaints that ignore what most people learn about civics in middle school.

在 HN 中查看
jst1fthsdys 2026/04/08 03:56
你是想说大多数民主党人不支持这场战争吗？因为他们确实这么做了。

另外，您是否会忽视媒体在最初的袭击之前所进行的宣传？或者说他们现在仍在气喘吁吁地进行宣传和自我审查？

整个统治阶级对此负有责任。执行该行动的军方对此负责。任何掌握权力的人没有采取任何措施来阻止这种情况，而是发布了一些措辞强硬的推文，那么他们就要承担责任。

Are you trying to say that the majority of dems don't support this war? Cause they do.

Also are you going to ignore the propaganda pushed by the press leading up to the initial attack? Or that they are still breathlessly parroting propaganda and self censoring right now?

The entire ruling class is responsible for this. The military carrying it out is responsible for it. Anyone in positions of power that have done nothing to stop this but post some strongly worded tweets is responsible.

在 HN 中查看
nathanaldensr 2026/04/08 02:51
美国政府几十年来一直无法控制人民。也许甚至比那更长。哪个“政党”控制并不重要。我们都知道这场战争为什么会发生，谁受益，谁在游说，他们收买了谁，以及特朗普向谁负责。我们都知道，但人们不敢说出来，因为一旦他们被针对说出来，他们就会失败。投票并没有阻止它。党对国会的控制并没有阻止它。总统们并没有阻止它。它正在发生，这就是一直以来的计划 两个都 派对。
The US government hasn't been in control of the People for decades and decades. Maybe even longer than that. It doesn't matter what "parties" have been in control. We all know why this war is happening, who benefits, who's lobbying for it, who they bought off, and who Trump answers to. We all know, but people are afraid to say it because they stand to lose once they're targeted for saying it. Voting hasn't stopped it. Party control of Congress hasn't stopped it. Presidents haven't stopped it. It's happening and this was the plan all along from both parties.

akagusu 2026/04/08 03:23

sheikhnbake 2026/04/08 02:06

媒体一直在大量报道特朗普的言论和战争。

The fatal flaw in our separation of powers is that even if congressional dems had a super majority, congress is powerless to enforce anything.

And the press has been covering Trump's rhetoric and the war pretty heavily.

lebuffon 2026/04/08 02:08

kubb 2026/04/08 02:14

这一定是因为演员不好。

How could it be that a political system which remained largely structurally unchanged since the freaking 18th century isn't equipped to deal with everything that has been going on in the world since then?

It must be due to bad actors.

Mr_Eri_Atlov 2026/04/08 03:18

sheikhnbake 2026/04/08 02:10

akagusu 2026/04/08 03:28

   > And the press has been covering Trump's rhetoric and the war pretty heavily.

在 HN 中查看
sheikhnbake 2026/04/08 03:40

You're preaching to the choir. The oligarchy has indeed thoroughly captured the fourth estate
在 HN 中查看
akagusu 2026/04/08 04:14
我认为他们已经捕获了所有。
I think they already captured all.
在 HN 中查看
markoman 2026/04/08 04:13
真正令人震惊的是，特朗普在 2024 年当选（以及现在这个混乱的世界）的很大一部分原因是共和党在选举期间通过本地市场广告购买巧妙地煽动了对跨性别主义的恐惧。正是这种玩法是特朗普在黑人男性和西班牙裔/拉丁裔等各种选民中取得胜利的大部分原因。这些人中的许多人完全是由“修饰”和女孩必须在运动中与变性女孩竞争的想法引发的。事实上，人们愿意选举特朗普，这样极少数女性就不必与跨性别者竞争，这是难以言表的悲伤。也许这（将变成）一个很好的教训，尽管选民的记忆力是出了名的短。群众中非常缺乏这种成熟度。

统治阶级把我们放在他们想要我们的地方——如此专注并担心失去我们的小工作，以至于我们没有其他太多的背景，因此我们可以作为选民做深思熟虑的工作。正如他们现在所知，这就是选举唐纳德·特朗普的方式。

What's truly shocking is that a large part of what got Trump elected in 2024 (and the world in this mess now) was that the GOP cleverly stoked the fears of transgenderism via local-market ad buys during the election. This very play was the reason for much of Trump's gains with various demo's such as black males and hispanics/latinos. So many of these folks were completely triggered by 'grooming' and the ideas that girls are having to compete against transgender girls in their sports. The fact that folks were willing to elect Trump so that a very small number of women wouldn't have to compete against transgenders, is sad beyond words. Perhaps this (will turn into) a lesson well learned, although voters have notoriously short memories. Sophistication of this type among the masses is sorely lacking.

The ruling class has us just where they want us -- so focused on & fearful of losing our little jobs that we don't have context around much else so that we can do a thoughtful job of performing as an electorate. This is how you elect a Donald Trump, as THEY have now learned.

在 HN 中查看
elzbardico 2026/04/08 06:51
我遇到的大多数投票给特朗普的人甚至可能都同意这一点，但投票给特朗普并不是因为那个无关紧要的小市民崇拜问题，而是因为，可笑的是，他们认为特朗普将成为工薪阶层和中产阶级的捍卫者。此外，他们中的很多人还搞笑地认为，当选特朗普将意味着不再用节省下来的钱来解决住房和基础设施等问题。
Most people I met who voted for trump may even agree with this, but voted for Trump not because of that irrelevant petit burgeouise fetiche question, but because, hilariously, they thought Trump would be a champion for the working poor and middle classes. Also, a lot of them also, hilariously, thought electing Trump would mean no more wars with the saved money used to save issues like housing and infrastructure.
在 HN 中查看
Barrin92 2026/04/08 02:14
甚至将此视为党派问题也是错误的。当一个民主国家的领导人用种族灭绝来威胁一个拥有9000万人口的国家时，该政府就应该在同一天下台。整个美国人口在哪里？除了蟋蟀的叫声，你什么也听不到，这是一个多么被动、病入膏肓的社会啊。
It's wrong to even frame this as a partisan question. When the leader of a democratic country threatens a nation of 90 million people with genocide, that should be the end of that government on that same day. Where is the entire US population? You hear nothing but crickets, what an utterly passive, terminally ill society.
在 HN 中查看
sheikhnbake 2026/04/08 02:24
每次发生的抗议活动都在稳步打破记录。自他上任以来，抗议活动在全国各地不断发生。这一切都已被广泛宣传
Protests have been steadily breaking records each time they occur. Protests have been occurring across the country since he took office. This has all been widely publicized
在 HN 中查看
dylan604 2026/04/08 02:43
我见过的事情正常运转的最好例子是韩国罢免了宣布戒严的领导人。韩国人对此表示不，并罢免了政府首脑。他们只是有道德上的毅力去做这件事。
The best example I've seen of things working correctly was with South Korea removing the leader that declared martial law. The South Koreans said nope to that, and removed the head of their government. They just had the moral fortitude to do it.
在 HN 中查看
srean 2026/04/08 03:23
那天他们让我感到非常自豪！
They made me incredibly proud that day !
在 HN 中查看
potsandpans 2026/04/08 06:03
你是认真地问还是只是把这个空间当作肥皂盒？

如果您是，这里有一些有用的信息：

美国的每个城市都有常备军，即警察部队。他们已做好准备并训练有素，可以暴力制止内乱。

警察部队部署先进的技术和拉网方法来跟踪所有参加抗议活动的公民并建立档案。

同样，媒体和现状被如此彻底地捕捉到，所有形式的大规模抗议似乎最终都会沦为对旁观者的无法无天的破坏，从而使暴力循环永久化。

国家对大规模逮捕没有任何问题。当文化时刻继续前进时，人们陷入了与极端指控的斗争中，并面临着不得不做出艰难的选择，请求恢复仅存的生活。如果你想战斗，就得冒着面临发达国家最不人道的条件的风险，在联邦监狱里呆上一段时间。

那么，“整个美国人口在哪里？”

大多数人都幸存并感到害怕。

Are you seriously asking or just using this space as a soapbox?

If you are, here's some useful info:

Every city in the United States has what could be categorized as a standing army in the form of a police force. They are ready and highly trained to violently stop civil unrest.

The police force deploys sophisticated technology and dragnet methodology to track and build profiles on all citizens that attend protests.

Similarly, the media and status quo is captured so thoroughly that all forms of mass protest appear eventually degenerate into lawless havoc to onlookers, which perpetuates a cycle of violence.

The state has no problem in engaging in mass arrests. And when the cultural moment has moved on, individuals are stuck fighting extreme charges and face having to make the difficult choice of pleaing out to resume what semblance of life remains. And if you want to fight, risk facing some of the most inhumane conditions in the developed world by spending some time in a federal prison.

So, "where is the entire US population?"

Mostly surviving and scared.

在 HN 中查看
elzbardico 2026/04/08 06:53
是的，为什么人们认为过去几十年来每个警察部门都能够购买军用车辆和装备？为了打击犯罪？哈哈
Yeah, why do people think every police department have been able to buy military vehicles and equipments for the last decades? To combat crime? LOL
在 HN 中查看
baal80spam 2026/04/08 02:14
帖子为何被错误引用？

它说：“整个文明今晚将消亡，永远不会再复活。”

将要。不可能。

Why is the post miquoted?

It says: "A whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again."

Will. Not might.

在 HN 中查看
dragonwriter 2026/04/08 02:18
不是引用，是总结。您引用了帖子的第一句话，但是该句子中隐含的确定性立即被下一句削弱了； “我不希望它发生，但它可能会发生”，而其余的则让事情变得更加混乱，继续说：“然而，既然我们已经完成了彻底的政权更迭，不同的、更聪明的、不那么激进的思想盛行，也许会发生一些革命性的奇妙事情，谁知道呢？今晚我们会发现，这是世界漫长而复杂的历史中最重要的时刻之一。47年的勒索、腐败和死亡，将最终结束。上帝保佑伟大的人民伊朗！”
It is not quoted, it is summarized. You are quoting the first sentence of the post, but the certainty implied in that sentence is immediately undercut by the next; “I don’t want it to happen but it probably will”, and made even more muddy by the rest, which continues: “However, now that we have Complete and Total Regime Change, where different, smarter, and less radicalized minds prevail, maybe something revolutionarily wonderful can happen, WHO KNOWS? We will find out tonight, one of the most important moments in the long and complex history of the World. 47 years of extortion, corruption, and death, will finally end. God Bless the Great People of Iran!”
在 HN 中查看
baal80spam 2026/04/08 02:20
谢谢，我想这是有道理的。
Thanks, I guess that makes sense.

w10-1 2026/04/08 02:10
无缘无故地破坏。

即使那些不关心无辜生命的人也认识到对其他人造成的可怕后果：

- 伊朗有权以高额保险无限期地对霍尔木兹海峡征税

- 俄罗斯有权进入中东，因为伊朗人将不得不拥抱他们唯一的盟友

特朗普的推动者负有责任。

Destruction for no good reason.

Even those who don't care about innocent lives recognize the horrible consequences for everyone else:

- Iran empowered to tax the strait of Hormuz indefinitely with high insurance

- Russia empowered with access to the middle east since the Iranians will have to embrace their only ally

Trump enablers are responsible.

AnimalMuppet 2026/04/08 01:42
唔。 你刚刚让我回忆起普京入侵乌克兰的事。 他说了所有这些威胁的话，并调动了所有这些军队，但是 一定 他永远不会做 那， 正确的？

事实证明，这是错误的。 有时候，咆哮并不是咆哮。 或者有时，咆哮者可能会用嘴把自己逼到墙角。

Hmm. You just gave me a flashback to Putin's invasion of Ukraine. He was saying all these threatening things, and moving all these troops, but surely he would never do that, right?

Wrong, it turned out. Sometimes bluster isn't bluster. Or perhaps sometimes blusterers back themselves into a corner with their mouth.

spwa4 2026/04/08 03:59
特朗普的威胁为何是这场战争中最极端的？天哪，“整个文明今晚可能会灭亡？”。伊朗伊斯兰政权50多年前就明确把消灭波斯文明作为自己的目标。他们为此付出了努力。或者怎么样：

伊朗政权威胁要让中东的生活变得根本不可能：

https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/23/iran-threat-to...

伊朗政权威胁要“在将伊朗还给伊朗人之前”杀死所有伊朗人：

https://www.politico.com/news/2026/01/16/no-sign-of-new-prot...

How is Trump's threat the most extreme in this war? Hell, "a whole civilization might die tonight?". The islamic regime in Iran has explicitly set wiping out Persian civilization as it's goal, more than 50 years ago. And they've worked hard at it. Or how about:

Iran's regime threatens to make life in the middle east essentially impossible:

https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/23/iran-threat-to...

Iran's regime threatens to kill every Iranian they can "before giving Iran back to Iranians":

https://www.politico.com/news/2026/01/16/no-sign-of-new-prot...
